title: New Drug Offers Hope by Delaying Type 1 Diabetes
subtitle: A breakthrough treatment allows patients to postpone the need for lifelong insulin therapy.

summary.en: A new drug called teplizumab has been approved for use in England and Wales. It is the first treatment that can delay the start of type 1 diabetes for up to three years. The medicine works by training the immune system to stop attacking the body's own cells. Experts describe this as a major step forward in managing the condition. However, patients must be identified early to receive the treatment.
summary.zh: 一款名為 teplizumab 的新藥已獲准在英格蘭與威爾斯使用。這是首款能將第一型糖尿病發作延緩長達三年的治療方式。該藥物透過訓練免疫系統停止攻擊自身細胞來發揮作用。專家形容這是管理該疾病的一大進步。然而，患者必須儘早被篩檢出來，才能接受此項治療。

paragraphs.en: A new medical treatment has been approved for use in England and Wales, marking a major change in how doctors handle type 1 diabetes. For the first time in over a century, a medicine is available that can delay the start of this condition. The drug, called teplizumab, does not cure the disease, but it can give patients up to three extra years before they need to start daily insulin treatment.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭與威爾斯核准了一項新的醫療方式，這標誌著醫師處理第一型糖尿病的方式出現了重大轉變。這是超過一個世紀以來，首次出現能延緩該疾病發作的藥物。這種名為「teplizumab」的藥物雖無法治癒疾病，但能讓患者在必須開始每日施打胰島素之前，多出長達三年的緩衝期。

paragraphs.en: Type 1 diabetes is a serious health condition where the body’s immune system attacks the cells in the pancreas that produce insulin. Insulin is a hormone that helps the body turn sugar into energy. Without it, people must monitor their blood sugar levels constantly and use injections or a pump to survive. According to The Guardian, this new drug is the biggest breakthrough in tackling the disease for more than 100 years. Before this, the only available treatment was insulin, which was discovered over a century ago and only replaces what the body is missing rather than fixing the cause of the problem.
paragraphs.zh: 第一型糖尿病是一種嚴重的健康狀況，患者的免疫系統會攻擊胰臟中負責製造胰島素的細胞。胰島素是一種能協助身體將糖分轉化為能量的荷爾蒙。若缺乏胰島素，患者必須持續監測血糖濃度，並透過注射或使用幫浦來維持生命。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這款新藥是過去一百多年來對抗該疾病最重大的突破。在此之前，唯一的治療方式是超過一世紀前發現的胰島素，其作用僅是補充身體缺失的物質，而非解決問題的根源。

paragraphs.en: Teplizumab is a type of immunotherapy, which is a treatment that helps the immune system work correctly. It works by training the immune system to stop attacking the cells in the pancreas. According to the BBC, the drug is given through a drip into a vein once a day for 14 days. After this two-week period, the treatment is finished. The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (Nice) approved the drug for adults and children aged eight and older who are in the early stages of the disease but do not yet have symptoms.
paragraphs.zh: Teplizumab 屬於免疫療法，這是一種協助免疫系統正常運作的治療方式。其原理是訓練免疫系統停止攻擊胰臟細胞。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，該藥物需透過靜脈點滴給藥，每日一次，療程為期十四天。兩週療程結束後，治療即告完成。英國國家健康與照顧卓越研究院（Nice）已核准該藥物用於八歲以上、處於疾病早期階段但尚未出現症狀的成人與兒童。

paragraphs.en: For many families, this news brings a sense of relief. Managing type 1 diabetes is a full-time job that requires careful planning of every meal and constant worry about blood sugar levels. Vicky, a mother whose son was diagnosed at age four, explained that the condition is something a family can never switch off from. She noted that parents must weigh food and count carbohydrates to give the right amount of insulin. Her husband, Ben, added that having three extra years of childhood without the daily burden of the disease would be a huge change for any family.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多家庭而言，這則消息帶來了一絲寬慰。管理第一型糖尿病是一項全職工作，需要仔細規劃每一餐，並時刻擔憂血糖濃度。一位兒子在四歲時確診的母親薇琪（Vicky）解釋說，這種疾病讓家庭永遠無法鬆懈。她指出，家長必須秤量食物並計算碳水化合物，以給予正確劑量的胰島素。她的丈夫班（Ben）補充道，若能讓孩子多出三年不用承受每日疾病負擔的童年，對任何家庭來說都是巨大的改變。

paragraphs.en: Experts in the field are very positive about the approval. Dr. Elizabeth Robertson, a director at the charity Diabetes UK, stated that this is a landmark moment. She explained that for the first time, doctors are moving beyond just using insulin and are now using a medicine that targets the root cause of the condition. Karen Addington, the chief executive of the charity Breakthrough T1D, described the potential impact as momentous. She said that if it were your own child, you would want to do everything possible to give them more years without the daily pressure of managing this relentless condition.
paragraphs.zh: 該領域的專家對此核准結果持高度正面態度。英國糖尿病慈善機構（Diabetes UK）的主管伊莉莎白·羅伯森（Elizabeth Robertson）博士表示，這是一個里程碑時刻。她解釋說，醫師首次跨越了僅使用胰島素的侷限，轉而使用能針對疾病根源的藥物。慈善機構「突破第一型糖尿病」（Breakthrough T1D）的執行長凱倫·艾丁頓（Karen Addington）形容其潛在影響意義深遠。她表示，如果是自己的孩子，一定會盡一切可能讓他們多出幾年時間，免於每日應對這種無情疾病的壓力。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement, there are challenges regarding how to find patients who can benefit from the drug. Because the medicine must be given before symptoms appear, patients need to be tested early. Currently, the UK does not have a national screening program to check children for the disease. Most people only find out they are at risk through tests done for other reasons or because the condition runs in their family. According to the BBC, Italy is currently the only country in the world that has a national screening program for young people. Diabetes charities in the UK are now campaigning for similar testing to be made available.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管各界感到振奮，但在尋找適合使用該藥物的患者方面仍存在挑戰。由於藥物必須在症狀出現前給予，因此患者需要儘早接受檢測。目前英國並無全國性的篩檢計畫來檢查兒童是否患病。大多數人僅是因其他原因進行檢查，或因家族病史才發現自己處於風險之中。根據 BBC 報導，義大利是目前全球唯一針對年輕族群實施全國篩檢計畫的國家。英國的糖尿病慈善機構目前正積極爭取推動類似的檢測服務。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the goal is to make sure that everyone who is eligible can access the treatment. The Guardian reports that Sanofi, the company that makes the drug, has reached a commercial agreement with the NHS to provide the medicine at a lower, confidential price. While this is a significant step forward, experts emphasize that early detection remains the key to unlocking these benefits. As researchers continue to study the condition, many hope that this is just the beginning of a future where type 1 diabetes might one day be prevented entirely.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，目標是確保所有符合資格的患者都能獲得治療。據《衛報》報導，藥廠賽諾菲（Sanofi）已與英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS）達成商業協議，將以更低且保密的價格提供藥物。雖然這是一大進步，但專家強調，早期發現仍是發揮藥物效益的關鍵。隨著研究人員持續深入研究該疾病，許多人希望這僅是一個開端，未來有一天或許能完全預防第一型糖尿病。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To manage, deal with, or take care of a situation or problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（把手），這裡作動詞用，指處理醫療狀況。文中：marking a major change in how doctors handle type 1 diabetes.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop thinking about something or to relax and stop focusing on a task.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 停止思考、放鬆、不再掛心。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指無法從照顧病患的壓力中抽離。文中：Vicky, a mother whose son was diagnosed at age four, explained that the condition is something a family can never switch off from.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To measure how heavy something is, often to ensure the correct amount for a specific purpose.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指為了計算胰島素劑量而精確測量食物重量。文中：She noted that parents must weigh food and count carbohydrates to give the right amount of insulin.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be a common characteristic or condition shared by many members of a family due to genetics.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個固定慣用語，字面意思容易誤解，指疾病具有家族遺傳性。文中：Most people only find out they are at risk through tests done for other reasons or because the condition runs in their family.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: First drug to delay onset of type 1 diabetes made available on NHS

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Drug that delays onset of type 1 diabetes approved in England and Wales

quiz.question: According to the article, how is the administration of teplizumab carried out for patients?

options.A: It is taken as a daily oral pill for a duration of three years.
options.B: It is injected into the bloodstream via a drip once daily for two weeks.
options.C: It is given as a monthly injection to help the pancreas produce insulin.
options.D: It is a permanent treatment that requires constant monitoring by doctors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，文中明確提到該藥物是透過點滴（drip）進入靜脈，每天一次，持續 14 天（兩週）。選項 A 錯誤，因為它不是口服藥，且療程僅兩週而非三年；選項 C 錯誤，因為它不是為了補充胰島素；選項 D 錯誤，因為療程結束後即完成，並非永久持續的治療。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current challenge of implementing this new treatment in the UK?

options.A: The drug is too expensive for the NHS to purchase even at a discounted price.
options.B: Most families are unwilling to have their children tested for diabetes.
options.C: The lack of a systematic screening process makes it difficult to identify eligible patients.
options.D: Doctors are hesitant to prescribe the drug because it has not been fully approved for children.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，文中指出該藥物必須在症狀出現前使用，但英國目前缺乏全國性的篩檢計畫，導致很難找出潛在患者。選項 A 與文章提到的商業協議相矛盾；選項 B 文中未提及家庭意願；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章第三段明確指出該藥物已獲准用於八歲及以上的兒童。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'relentless' mean as used in the context of managing type 1 diabetes?

options.A: Occurring only at specific, predictable intervals.
options.B: Constantly persistent and difficult to stop or escape.
options.C: Causing sudden and short-lived physical pain.
options.D: Easily managed with modern medical technology.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在第五段中，Karen Addington 形容糖尿病是一種 relentless 的狀況。根據上下文，糖尿病需要全天候的血糖監測與飲食規劃，無法中斷，因此這裡的 relentless 指的是「持續不斷且難以擺脫的」。選項 A 與原文意思相反；選項 C 錯誤，因為它不是短暫的；選項 D 與文中對疾病負擔的描述不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the approval of teplizumab?

options.A: It is a complete cure that will eventually replace the need for insulin injections entirely.
options.B: It represents a significant medical advancement in delaying type 1 diabetes, despite logistical challenges.
options.C: It is a controversial treatment that has faced heavy criticism from medical experts in the UK.
options.D: It is primarily a financial success for the pharmaceutical company Sanofi rather than a health benefit.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章核心在於介紹 teplizumab 作為一種能延緩第一型糖尿病發作的新療法，雖然它是重大的醫學突破，但文章後半段也誠實地討論了篩檢與執行上的困難。選項 A 誇大了療效，文中強調它不能治癒疾病；選項 C 與文中專家（如 Dr. Elizabeth Robertson）的正面態度不符；選項 D 偏離主題，文章重點在於對病患的影響而非商業利益。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
